turns.content: My boss reminds me of my dad. I shut down around him — like, completely go offline — and I'm ready to look at this. I think.
That "I think" at the end — what's that about?

*laughs a little*

Yeah, I caught that too. I guess... I'm ready in the sense that I know it's costing me. At work, in my life. But "ready" might be generous. More like — I'm tired of not being ready, so I'm here.

That's an honest place to start from.

What's it costing you?

Professionally, a lot. I mean, I'm good at my job — I know I'm good at my job — but the moment my boss walks into a room, something just... collapses. I can't think clearly, I can't speak up, I second-guess everything I was about to say. And then afterwards I'm furious at myself, which costs me too.

And I think it's costing me something harder to name. Like... a version of myself I can't access when it matters. That's the part that bothers me more, honestly.
That version of yourself — the one you can't access — what's she like?

She's... clear. Not loud, not aggressive, just — clear. She knows what she thinks and she says it. She doesn't shrink.

*pause*

I know she exists because I can be her in other contexts. With peers, with friends, even with some people who have authority over me but don't... trigger the thing. So it's not like she's gone. She just goes somewhere I can't reach when he's in the room.

*small pause*

That's a good question. I've thought about it a lot, actually. My best theory is that she... hides? Like something in me decides it's not safe to be visible, and she just — tucks back. Goes quiet.

And I know that's the dad thing. I know it intellectually. When I was growing up, being visible — having opinions, taking up space — that wasn't... it didn't go well. So I learned to make myself small. And apparently I never fully unlearned it.

*shifts slightly*

It's interesting, I can talk about this pretty analytically. I've done some work on it. But knowing the origin doesn't seem to change the reflex.

Yeah. Understanding it and feeling it are different rooms.

What's happening in your body right now, as you talk about this?

*pauses, checks in*

Honestly? Pretty okay right now. A little... activated, maybe. Like a low hum. Nothing intense.

When I'm actually in the situation with my boss it's different — it's more like a full-body thing, like I just... drop out. But right now talking about it I feel mostly fine.

*slight hesitation*

Ask me again when we get closer to the actual — to him. To my dad. I have a feeling it'll be different then.
That low hum — can you feel where it is in your body right now?

*takes a moment*

Yeah. Chest, I think. Sort of... upper chest. Not painful, just — present. Like something sitting there quietly.

*pause*

It's interesting, I don't usually notice it until someone asks. It's just kind of... background. Like it's always there and I've stopped registering it.

Don't try to change it or figure it out. Just let it be there.

What does it feel like it's holding?
